description: Cargo workspace initializes and compiles with all crate stubs (tilde-core, tilde-server, tilde-cli, tilde-dav, tilde-cal, tilde-card, tilde-photos, tilde-notes, tilde-collections, tilde-email, tilde-mcp, tilde-backup, tilde-notify)
steps: Step 1: Run `cargo check` in workspace root
Step 2: Verify all 13 crates compile without errors
Step 3: Verify workspace Cargo.toml lists all crate members

description: Binary produces a single executable named 'tilde' via cargo build
steps: Step 1: Run `cargo build --release`
Step 2: Verify target/release/tilde binary exists
Step 3: Verify binary runs and prints help with `./tilde --help`

description: Config loading via figment supports layered TOML + env + CLI flags
steps: Step 1: Create a minimal config.toml with [server] hostname = 'test.example.com'
Step 2: Set TILDE_HOSTNAME=env.example.com environment variable
Step 3: Run tilde with --config pointing to the TOML file
Step 4: Verify env var overrides TOML value
Step 5: Run tilde with explicit CLI flag for hostname
Step 6: Verify CLI flag overrides env var

description: XDG Base Directory compliance in user mode: config, data, cache, runtime dirs resolve correctly
steps: Step 1: Unset STATE_DIRECTORY to ensure user mode
Step 2: Set XDG_CONFIG_HOME to a temp directory
Step 3: Run tilde status or init
Step 4: Verify config is read from $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/tilde/config.toml
Step 5: Verify data dir defaults to $XDG_DATA_HOME/tilde/
Step 6: Verify cache dir defaults to $XDG_CACHE_HOME/tilde/

description: Systemd mode detection: when STATE_DIRECTORY is set, paths resolve to /var/lib/tilde etc.
steps: Step 1: Set STATE_DIRECTORY=/var/lib/tilde
Step 2: Run tilde in systemd mode
Step 3: Verify config path resolves to /etc/tilde/config.toml
Step 4: Verify data dir resolves to /var/lib/tilde/
Step 5: Verify cache dir resolves to /var/cache/tilde/

description: SQLite database initialization with WAL mode, FTS5, JSON1, and foreign keys
steps: Step 1: Run tilde init to create database
Step 2: Connect to tilde.db with sqlite3
Step 3: Verify PRAGMA journal_mode returns 'wal'
Step 4: Verify PRAGMA foreign_keys returns 1
Step 5: Verify FTS5 is available by checking notes_fts table creation

description: SQLite busy_timeout set to 5000ms and synchronous=NORMAL
steps: Step 1: Start tilde server
Step 2: Connect to database
Step 3: Verify PRAGMA busy_timeout returns 5000
Step 4: Verify PRAGMA synchronous returns 1 (NORMAL)

description: Migration runner applies SQL migrations in version order with checksum verification
steps: Step 1: Start with fresh database
Step 2: Run tilde with migrations
Step 3: Verify migrations table exists with version, name, applied_at, checksum columns
Step 4: Verify all migrations applied in order
Step 5: Run tilde again and verify no migrations re-applied
Step 6: Modify a migration SQL file and verify checksum mismatch is detected

description: Argon2id password hashing: admin password from TILDE_ADMIN_PASSWORD is hashed on first use
steps: Step 1: Set TILDE_ADMIN_PASSWORD=testpass123
Step 2: Run tilde init
Step 3: Verify password is stored as Argon2id hash in database (not plaintext)
Step 4: Verify hash starts with $argon2id$ prefix
Step 5: Verify the env var value can authenticate via HTTP Basic Auth
Step 6: Verify wrong password is rejected with 401
Step 7: Verify empty password is rejected
Step 8: Verify timing of valid vs invalid password check is similar
Step 9: Verify password hash uses unique salt per hash
Step 10: Verify re-running init with same password produces different hash (unique salt)

description: Opaque session tokens with 24h sliding TTL, stored in auth_sessions table
steps: Step 1: Authenticate with admin password
Step 2: Receive session token in format tilde_session_<32 random bytes>
Step 3: Verify token is exactly 46 chars (prefix + 32 random bytes)
Step 4: Verify session record in auth_sessions table
Step 5: Verify token_prefix contains first 8 chars of random portion
Step 6: Verify created_at timestamp is current
Step 7: Verify expires_at is 24h from created_at
Step 8: Make a request using the session token
Step 9: Verify last_used_at is updated to current time
Step 10: Verify expires_at slides forward by 24h from last use
Step 11: Wait until session expired, verify token rejected

description: Constant-time token comparison using subtle crate for session validation
steps: Step 1: Authenticate and get session token
Step 2: Verify valid token authenticates
Step 3: Verify token with 1 char difference is rejected
Step 4: Verify timing of valid vs invalid token comparison is similar (constant-time)

description: Rate limiting on auth endpoints: 5 attempts per 15 minutes per IP
steps: Step 1: Send 1st failed login attempt → verify 401 response
Step 2: Send 2nd failed login attempt → verify 401 response
Step 3: Send 3rd failed login attempt → verify 401 response
Step 4: Send 4th failed login attempt → verify 401 response
Step 5: Send 5th failed login attempt → verify 401 response
Step 6: Send 6th failed login attempt → verify 429 Too Many Requests (lockout)
Step 7: Verify lockout response includes Retry-After header
Step 8: Attempt valid login during lockout → verify still locked out
Step 9: Wait for lockout_duration_minutes (15 min) to expire
Step 10: Verify valid login succeeds after lockout expires
Step 11: Verify different IP address is not affected by lockout

description: App-password creation with scope prefix restricting access to URL paths
steps: Step 1: Run `tilde auth app-password create --name phone-dav --scope /dav/*`
Step 2: Verify app-password is displayed to user only once
Step 3: Verify app-password works for GET /dav/files/ via Basic Auth
Step 4: Verify app-password works for PUT /dav/files/test.txt
Step 5: Verify app-password works for PROPFIND /dav/files/
Step 6: Verify app-password is rejected for GET /caldav/
Step 7: Verify app-password is rejected for POST /mcp/
Step 8: Verify app-password hash stored as Argon2id in app_passwords table
Step 9: Verify scope_prefix stored correctly in database
Step 10: Verify app-password last_used_at updates on use

description: App-password list and revoke via CLI
steps: Step 1: Create two app-passwords
Step 2: Run `tilde auth app-password list`
Step 3: Verify both passwords shown with name, scope, created_at, last_used_at
Step 4: Run `tilde auth app-password revoke <id>`
Step 5: Verify revoked password no longer authenticates
Step 6: Verify remaining password still works

description: Password reset via SSH: `tilde auth reset-password` changes the admin password
steps: Step 1: Set initial password and authenticate
Step 2: Run `tilde auth reset-password`
Step 3: Enter new password
Step 4: Verify old password no longer works
Step 5: Verify new password authenticates

description: Session list and revoke via CLI
steps: Step 1: Create multiple sessions by authenticating from different user agents
Step 2: Run `tilde auth session list`
Step 3: Verify sessions shown with token_prefix, user_agent, source_ip, last_used_at
Step 4: Run `tilde auth session revoke <id>`
Step 5: Verify revoked session no longer authenticates

description: axum HTTP server starts and serves HTTPS with rustls on port 443
steps: Step 1: Configure tilde with TLS mode = acme (or manual with test certs)
Step 2: Start `tilde serve`
Step 3: Verify HTTPS listener on configured port
Step 4: Verify TLS handshake completes with valid certificate

description: TLS upstream mode: server speaks plain HTTP when reverse proxy handles TLS
steps: Step 1: Set tls.mode = 'upstream' and server.listen_port = 8080
Step 2: Start tilde serve
Step 3: Verify HTTP listener on port 8080 (no TLS)
Step 4: Verify requests with X-Forwarded-Proto: https are accepted
Step 5: Verify trusted_proxies filtering works

description: Health endpoint returns JSON with db status, disk usage, and uptime
steps: Step 1: Start tilde serve
Step 2: GET /health
Step 3: Verify 200 OK response with JSON body
Step 4: Verify JSON contains db_status, disk_usage, uptime fields
Step 5: Verify db_status reflects actual database connectivity

description: Metrics endpoint returns Prometheus format and is localhost-only
steps: Step 1: Start tilde serve
Step 2: GET /metrics from localhost
Step 3: Verify 200 OK with Prometheus text format
Step 4: Attempt GET /metrics from non-localhost IP
Step 5: Verify non-localhost request is rejected

description: Structured JSON logging via tracing + tracing-subscriber
steps: Step 1: Start tilde serve with logging.format = 'json'
Step 2: Make several requests
Step 3: Verify log output is valid JSON per line
Step 4: Verify log entries contain timestamp, level, message, span context

description: sd-notify systemd integration with Type=notify and watchdog
steps: Step 1: Start tilde serve under systemd with Type=notify
Step 2: Verify systemd receives READY=1 notification
Step 3: Verify watchdog pings at WatchdogSec/2 interval
Step 4: Kill tilde process and verify systemd detects failure

description: SIGHUP handler reloads logging.level, mcp.tool_allowlist, mcp.default_rate_limit, notifications, backup schedule
steps: Step 1: Start tilde serve with logging.level = 'info'
Step 2: Modify config.toml to logging.level = 'debug'
Step 3: Send SIGHUP to tilde process
Step 4: Verify log level changes to debug without restart
Step 5: Verify non-hot-reloadable settings (server.*, tls.*) are NOT reloaded

description: HSTS header with preload on all responses
steps: Step 1: Make any request to tilde server
Step 2: Verify Strict-Transport-Security header present
Step 3: Verify header includes max-age, includeSubDomains, preload

description: Host header allowlist rejects requests for unknown hosts
steps: Step 1: Configure hostname as cloud.example.com
Step 2: Send request with Host: cloud.example.com
Step 3: Verify request is accepted
Step 4: Send request with Host: evil.example.com
Step 5: Verify request is rejected

description: tilde init interactive wizard creates config, sets password, generates backup key, creates directories, runs migrations
steps: Step 1: Run `tilde init` in a fresh environment
Step 2: Provide hostname when prompted
Step 3: Set admin password when prompted
Step 4: Choose TLS mode
Step 5: Verify config.toml is created with provided values
Step 6: Verify password is hashed and stored
Step 7: Verify backup encryption keypair is generated
Step 8: Verify recovery code is printed to terminal
Step 9: Verify data directories are created (files, photos, calendars, contacts, mail, etc.)
Step 10: Verify SQLite migrations are applied
Step 11: Verify init does NOT auto-enable or start systemd service

description: tilde status shows server state, disk usage, last backup, sync status, cert expiry
steps: Step 1: Start tilde serve
Step 2: Run `tilde status`
Step 3: Verify output includes server state (running/stopped)
Step 4: Verify output includes disk usage for data directory
Step 5: Verify output includes last backup timestamp
Step 6: Verify output includes certificate expiry date
Step 7: Verify --json flag produces valid JSON output

description: tilde diagnose validates config, connectivity, cert, disk, and dependencies
steps: Step 1: Run `tilde diagnose`
Step 2: Verify it checks config file validity
Step 3: Verify it checks network connectivity
Step 4: Verify it checks TLS certificate status
Step 5: Verify it checks disk space availability
Step 6: Verify it checks for exiftool, libheif, ffmpeg dependencies
Step 7: Verify it reports missing dependencies clearly

description: Shell completion generation for bash, zsh, and fish
steps: Step 1: Run `tilde completions bash`
Step 2: Verify valid bash completion script is output
Step 3: Run `tilde completions zsh` and verify zsh completions
Step 4: Run `tilde completions fish` and verify fish completions

description: WebDAV OPTIONS returns DAV: 1 header and supported methods
steps: Step 1: Send OPTIONS request to /dav/files/
Step 2: Verify response includes DAV: 1 header
Step 3: Verify Allow header lists: OPTIONS, GET, PUT, DELETE, MKCOL, MOVE, COPY, PROPFIND, PROPPATCH, HEAD

description: WebDAV GET downloads a file with correct content-type and supports Range headers
steps: Step 1: Upload a test file via PUT
Step 2: GET the file and verify content matches
Step 3: Verify Content-Type header matches file MIME type
Step 4: Send GET with Range: bytes=0-99 header
Step 5: Verify 206 Partial Content with correct byte range

description: WebDAV PUT uploads a file atomically (tmp → fsync → rename → fsync parent dir)
steps: Step 1: PUT a new file to /dav/files/test.txt with text content 'hello world'
Step 2: Verify 201 Created response with Location header
Step 3: Verify file exists on disk at correct path
Step 4: Verify file content matches uploaded content exactly
Step 5: Verify ETag header in response is non-empty
Step 6: Verify Content-Type header in response
Step 7: Verify files table record created with correct size_bytes
Step 8: Verify files table record has correct content_type
Step 9: Verify no orphaned tmp file remains after write
Step 10: PUT a second file and verify 201 Created

description: WebDAV PUT with If-Match ETag returns 412 on conflict
steps: Step 1: Upload file and note its ETag
Step 2: Upload again with correct If-Match → verify 204 No Content
Step 3: Upload with wrong If-Match → verify 412 Precondition Failed
Step 4: Verify original file is not modified after 412

description: WebDAV DELETE removes files and collections
steps: Step 1: Create a text file via PUT /dav/files/deleteme.txt
Step 2: Verify file exists via GET
Step 3: DELETE /dav/files/deleteme.txt
Step 4: Verify 204 No Content response
Step 5: Verify GET returns 404
Step 6: Verify file removed from disk
Step 7: Verify files table record removed
Step 8: MKCOL /dav/files/testcol/
Step 9: PUT 2 files into testcol/
Step 10: DELETE /dav/files/testcol/
Step 11: Verify collection and all children removed from disk and database

description: WebDAV MKCOL creates collections (directories)
steps: Step 1: MKCOL /dav/files/testdir/
Step 2: Verify 201 Created response
Step 3: Verify directory exists on disk
Step 4: MKCOL on existing collection → verify 405 Method Not Allowed
Step 5: MKCOL with missing parent → verify 409 Conflict

description: WebDAV MOVE renames/moves files and collections, updates oc:id mapping
steps: Step 1: Create a file and note its oc:id
Step 2: MOVE file to new path with Destination header
Step 3: Verify file at new path, old path returns 404
Step 4: Verify oc:id is preserved (same UUID)
Step 5: MOVE a collection with children and verify recursive move

description: WebDAV COPY duplicates files and collections
steps: Step 1: Create a file with content
Step 2: COPY file to new path with Destination header
Step 3: Verify both source and destination exist
Step 4: Verify destination has different oc:id (new UUID)
Step 5: Verify content is identical

description: WebDAV PROPFIND Depth: 0 returns properties for single resource
steps: Step 1: Upload a file
Step 2: PROPFIND with Depth: 0 on the file
Step 3: Verify XML response contains getcontentlength, getcontenttype, getetag, getlastmodified
Step 4: Verify oc:id, oc:fileid, oc:permissions, oc:size custom properties

description: WebDAV PROPFIND Depth: 1 returns properties for collection and children
steps: Step 1: Create a collection via MKCOL /dav/files/testdir/
Step 2: PUT file1.txt (100 bytes) into testdir/
Step 3: PUT file2.md (200 bytes) into testdir/
Step 4: PUT file3.jpg (1000 bytes) into testdir/
Step 5: PROPFIND with Depth: 1 on testdir/
Step 6: Verify XML multistatus response contains 4 entries (collection + 3 files)
Step 7: Verify collection entry has resourcetype = collection
Step 8: Verify each file has getcontentlength matching actual size
Step 9: Verify each file has correct getcontenttype
Step 10: Verify each file has getetag and getlastmodified
Step 11: Verify oc:id present for each entry

description: WebDAV PROPPATCH sets and removes custom properties
steps: Step 1: Upload a file
Step 2: PROPPATCH to set a custom property
Step 3: PROPFIND and verify custom property is returned
Step 4: PROPPATCH to remove the custom property
Step 5: PROPFIND and verify property is no longer returned

description: WebDAV HEAD returns metadata without body
steps: Step 1: Upload a file
Step 2: HEAD request to the file
Step 3: Verify response has Content-Length, Content-Type, ETag headers
Step 4: Verify response body is empty

description: Stable oc:id per file survives moves and renames (server-assigned UUID)
steps: Step 1: Upload file test.txt via PUT and note response ETag
Step 2: PROPFIND test.txt and extract oc:id (UUID)
Step 3: MKCOL /dav/files/subdir/
Step 4: MOVE test.txt to /dav/files/renamed.txt
Step 5: PROPFIND renamed.txt and verify oc:id is unchanged
Step 6: MOVE renamed.txt to /dav/files/subdir/renamed.txt
Step 7: PROPFIND subdir/renamed.txt and verify oc:id is still the same
Step 8: Verify ETag may have changed (different from step 1)
Step 9: Verify files table path column updated
Step 10: Verify old paths return 404

description: Nextcloud chunked upload v2: MKCOL session → chunk PUTs → MOVE finalize
steps: Step 1: MKCOL /dav/uploads/user/session-123/ to create upload session
Step 2: PUT chunk 1 to /dav/uploads/user/session-123/1
Step 3: PUT chunk 2 to /dav/uploads/user/session-123/2
Step 4: PUT chunk 3 to /dav/uploads/user/session-123/3
Step 5: MOVE session dir to /dav/files/largefile.bin with OC-Total-Length header
Step 6: Verify assembled file at destination matches original content
Step 7: Verify ETag returned in response
Step 8: Verify upload session directory is cleaned up
Step 9: Verify bytes were streamed (never buffered full file in memory)
Step 10: Verify per-session size tracking is correct

description: Chunked upload session expires after 24 hours and staging dir is cleaned up
steps: Step 1: Create upload session
Step 2: Upload 1 chunk but don't finalize
Step 3: Wait for session to expire (or simulate expiry)
Step 4: Verify session is marked expired in chunked_uploads table
Step 5: Verify staging directory is cleaned up on next startup

description: Chunked upload disk-space pre-check prevents uploads when disk is full
steps: Step 1: Create upload session with OC-Total-Length header
Step 2: Verify server checks available disk space
Step 3: If insufficient space, verify 507 Insufficient Storage response

description: Nextcloud OCS capabilities stub at /ocs/v2.php/cloud/capabilities returns minimal JSON
steps: Step 1: GET /ocs/v2.php/cloud/capabilities
Step 2: Verify 200 OK with JSON response
Step 3: Verify JSON declares WebDAV chunked upload support
Step 4: Verify format is compatible with Nextcloud clients

description: /status.php returns Nextcloud-compatible status JSON
steps: Step 1: GET /status.php
Step 2: Verify JSON response with installed: true, version: '28.0.0'
Step 3: Verify format satisfies Nextcloud client checks

description: /remote.php/dav/* aliases redirect to /dav/*
steps: Step 1: Send request to /remote.php/dav/files/test.txt
Step 2: Verify redirect to /dav/files/test.txt
Step 3: Send request to /remote.php/webdav/test.txt
Step 4: Verify redirect to /dav/files/test.txt

description: Nextcloud Login Flow v2: initiate, display login page, authenticate, poll for credentials
steps: Step 1: POST /login/v2 with no auth → receive 200 OK JSON
Step 2: Verify JSON has poll.token (string), poll.endpoint (URL), login (URL)
Step 3: Poll the endpoint immediately → receive 404 (not yet authenticated)
Step 4: GET the login URL → verify HTML login page served
Step 5: Verify login page has CSRF token in hidden field
Step 6: POST login form with wrong password → verify error displayed
Step 7: POST login form with correct password and CSRF token
Step 8: Poll the endpoint with token → receive 200 OK JSON
Step 9: Verify response contains server (URL), loginName (string), appPassword (string)
Step 10: Use appPassword for WebDAV Basic Auth GET /dav/files/ → verify 200 OK
Step 11: Verify appPassword is stored as app-password with /dav/* scope
Step 12: Poll again with same token → verify 404 (one-time use)

description: macOS Finder LOCK request handling: respond 200 OK (FakeLs pattern)
steps: Step 1: Send LOCK request to a WebDAV resource (as Finder would)
Step 2: Verify 200 OK response (not 405 or 501)
Step 3: Verify file operations work after LOCK response

description: Files table tracks UUID-based oc:id, path, metadata with proper indexes
steps: Step 1: Upload a file via WebDAV
Step 2: Query files table in SQLite
Step 3: Verify record with UUID id, correct path, parent_path, name, size_bytes, content_type, etag, sha256, hlc
Step 4: Verify is_directory field for directories vs files

description: CalDAV via RustiCal: calendar creation, modification, and deletion
steps: Step 1: MKCALENDAR request to create a new calendar
Step 2: Verify calendar exists via PROPFIND
Step 3: PROPPATCH to modify calendar display name
Step 4: DELETE calendar
Step 5: Verify calendar no longer exists

description: CalDAV event CRUD: create, read, update, delete VEVENT
steps: Step 1: Create calendar
Step 2: PUT a VEVENT ICS file to the calendar
Step 3: GET the event and verify ICS content
Step 4: PUT updated event with If-Match ETag
Step 5: Verify event is updated
Step 6: DELETE the event
Step 7: Verify event no longer exists

description: CalDAV VTODO tasks: create and manage tasks for Apple Reminders and jtx Board
steps: Step 1: Create a calendar for tasks
Step 2: PUT a VTODO ICS with summary, due date, priority, status
Step 3: GET and verify VTODO content
Step 4: Update VTODO status to COMPLETED
Step 5: Verify updated status persists

description: CalDAV REPORT: calendar-query returns events matching time range
steps: Step 1: Create calendar with 5 events spanning different dates
Step 2: Send calendar-query REPORT with time-range filter
Step 3: Verify only events within range are returned
Step 4: Verify returned data includes full VCALENDAR

description: CalDAV REPORT: calendar-multiget retrieves specific events by URL
steps: Step 1: Create 3 events in a calendar
Step 2: Send calendar-multiget REPORT requesting 2 specific event URLs
Step 3: Verify exactly 2 events returned
Step 4: Verify correct events returned with full VCALENDAR data

description: CalDAV sync-collection REPORT with sync-token for incremental sync (RFC 6578)
steps: Step 1: Create calendar with 2 initial events
Step 2: Send initial sync-collection REPORT with empty sync-token
Step 3: Verify all events returned in response
Step 4: Extract sync-token from response
Step 5: Add a new event to the calendar
Step 6: Send sync-collection with previous sync-token
Step 7: Verify only new event returned (not all events)
Step 8: Delete one of the original events
Step 9: Send sync-collection with latest sync-token
Step 10: Verify deleted event indicated in response (404 status for that href)

description: CalDAV getctag Apple extension reports calendar modification state
steps: Step 1: PROPFIND calendar to get getctag
Step 2: Add an event
Step 3: PROPFIND again and verify getctag changed

description: /.well-known/caldav redirects to /caldav/ (RFC 6764)
steps: Step 1: GET /.well-known/caldav
Step 2: Verify 301 redirect to /caldav/

description: /.well-known/carddav redirects to /carddav/ (RFC 6764)
steps: Step 1: GET /.well-known/carddav
Step 2: Verify 301 redirect to /carddav/

description: current-user-principal (RFC 5397) returns correct principal URL
steps: Step 1: PROPFIND /principals/user/ requesting current-user-principal
Step 2: Verify response contains principal URL

description: CardDAV addressbook CRUD: create, modify, delete addressbooks
steps: Step 1: Create addressbook via MKCOL with resourcetype addressbook
Step 2: PROPFIND to verify addressbook exists
Step 3: PROPPATCH to update display name
Step 4: DELETE addressbook
Step 5: Verify addressbook removed

description: CardDAV contact CRUD: create, read, update, delete VCARD
steps: Step 1: Create addressbook
Step 2: PUT a VCARD file with name, email, phone, org
Step 3: GET contact and verify VCARD content
Step 4: PUT updated VCARD with If-Match
Step 5: DELETE contact
Step 6: Verify contact removed

description: CardDAV addressbook-multiget REPORT retrieves specific contacts
steps: Step 1: Create 3 contacts in addressbook
Step 2: Send addressbook-multiget REPORT requesting 2 contacts
Step 3: Verify exactly 2 contacts returned with full VCARD data

description: CardDAV sync-collection REPORT with sync-token for incremental contact sync
steps: Step 1: Initial sync-collection → get sync-token
Step 2: Add new contact
Step 3: Sync with previous token → verify new contact returned
Step 4: Delete a contact
Step 5: Sync → verify deletion indicated

description: CalDAV/CardDAV auth integration: app-passwords and sessions work with RustiCal handlers
steps: Step 1: Create app-password scoped to /caldav/*
Step 2: Access CalDAV with app-password via Basic Auth → verify success
Step 3: Create app-password scoped to /carddav/*
Step 4: Access CardDAV with that password → verify success
Step 5: Verify cross-scope access is denied

description: CLI: tilde calendar list shows all calendars
steps: Step 1: Create 2 calendars via CalDAV
Step 2: Run `tilde calendar list`
Step 3: Verify both calendars listed with names

description: CLI: tilde calendar events lists events with optional date range filter
steps: Step 1: Create events spanning multiple dates
Step 2: Run `tilde calendar events`
Step 3: Verify all events listed
Step 4: Run `tilde calendar events --from 2025-01-01 --to 2025-01-31`
Step 5: Verify only events in range returned

description: CLI: tilde contacts list and tilde contacts search
steps: Step 1: Create several contacts via CardDAV
Step 2: Run `tilde contacts list` → verify all contacts listed
Step 3: Run `tilde contacts search 'John'` → verify matching contacts returned

description: Photo ingestion: file dropped in _inbox/ is auto-organized by date pattern
steps: Step 1: Configure organization_pattern = '{year}/{month:02}'
Step 2: Drop a JPEG with EXIF date 2025-06-15 into photos/_inbox/
Step 3: Wait for debounce (5s default) to pass
Step 4: Verify file size stability check completes
Step 5: Verify file type validated by magic bytes
Step 6: Verify ExifTool subprocess reads metadata
Step 7: Verify file moved to photos/2025/06/filename.jpg
Step 8: Verify original file removed from _inbox/
Step 9: Verify photos table record created with UUID
Step 10: Verify original_sha256 and current_sha256 computed and match
Step 11: Verify photo_tags entries created from EXIF keywords if present
Step 12: Verify thumbnail generation job enqueued in jobs table

description: Photo ingestion: _library-drop/ preserves user's directory structure
steps: Step 1: Create photos/_library-drop/vacation/beach/img.jpg
Step 2: Wait for processing
Step 3: Verify file remains at vacation/beach/img.jpg path (not reorganized)
Step 4: Verify database record created with correct path

description: Photo ingestion: _untriaged/ receives photos with insufficient metadata
steps: Step 1: Drop a JPEG without EXIF DateTimeOriginal into _inbox/
Step 2: Wait for processing
Step 3: Verify file moved to photos/_untriaged/
Step 4: Verify database record marks it as untriaged

description: Photo ingestion: _errors/ receives decode failures with error sidecar .txt
steps: Step 1: Drop a corrupt image file into _inbox/
Step 2: Wait for processing
Step 3: Verify file moved to photos/_errors/
Step 4: Verify error description sidecar .txt file created alongside

description: Photo ingestion validates file type by magic bytes, not just extension
steps: Step 1: Rename a text file to .jpg
Step 2: Drop into _inbox/
Step 3: Verify file is rejected (moved to _errors/) based on magic byte check
Step 4: Drop a valid JPEG with .txt extension into _inbox/
Step 5: Verify it's processed correctly as a JPEG

description: Photo ingestion debounce: waits 5 seconds and verifies file size is stable before processing
steps: Step 1: Start writing a large file to _inbox/
Step 2: Verify processing doesn't start while file size is changing
Step 3: Complete the write
Step 4: Verify processing starts after 5s debounce
Step 5: Verify complete file is processed (not partial)

description: Photo metadata read via ExifTool subprocess with configurable timeout
steps: Step 1: Drop a JPEG with rich EXIF data (camera, GPS, date, lens) into _inbox/
Step 2: Wait for ingestion to complete
Step 3: Query photos table for the imported photo
Step 4: Verify taken_at extracted from EXIF DateTimeOriginal
Step 5: Verify camera_make extracted
Step 6: Verify camera_model extracted
Step 7: Verify lens info extracted
Step 8: Verify focal_length_mm extracted
Step 9: Verify aperture (f-number) extracted
Step 10: Verify iso extracted
Step 11: Verify exposure_time extracted (e.g., '1/250')
Step 12: Verify gps_latitude and gps_longitude extracted
Step 13: Verify orientation extracted

description: Photo tag writing via ExifTool: XMP dc:Subject + IPTC Keywords

Step 8: Call notes.append → verify allowed
Step 9: Call calendar.create_event → verify still denied
